concepts.title: Soda lime Decarboxylation
concepts.conceptBody:
Reaction involving complete - COOH group 

(1) Soda lime decarboxylation: Sodium or potassium salts of carboxylic acids on heating with soda lime $(\text{NaOH}$ and $\text{CaO})$ give alkanes with one carbon less than the parent acids.

$\underset{Sodium\,salt\,ofacid}{\mathop{\text{RCOONa}+\text{NaOH}}}\,\xrightarrow{\text{CaO}}\underset{alkane}{\mathop{\text{R}-\text{H}+}}\,\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$

(2) Reduction

$R\,COOH\xrightarrow[{}]{LiAl{{H}_{4}}}R\,C{{H}_{2}}OH$

concepts.title: Reaction with Hydrazoic acid and Hunsdicker reaction
concepts.conceptBody:
Reaction with hydrazoic acid$\left( \text{H}{{\text{N}}_{3}} \right)$

(4) Brominative decarboxylation (Hunsdicker reaction)

$\text{R}-\text{COOH}\xrightarrow{\text{A}{{\text{g}}_{2}}\text{O}}\text{RCO}_{2}^{-}\text{A}{{\text{g}}^{+}}\xrightarrow[\text{in CC}{{\text{l}}_{4}}]{\text{B}{{\text{r}}_{2}}}\text{R}-\text{Br}+\text{AgBr}(\downarrow )+\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}(\uparrow )$

concepts.title: Heating of alpha, beta and gamma hydroxy acid
Decarboxylation
concepts.conceptBody:
Decarboxylation 

 $\beta $-keto acids on slightest warming alone or in presence of a base undergoes ready loss of $\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$. The process of loss of $\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$ is called “decarboxylation”. For example,

concepts.title: Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky Reaction
concepts.conceptBody:
Reactions involving R-Part 

Hell-Volhard Zelinsky (HVZ) reaction 

It is also the $\alpha $-Halogenation of aliphatic acids. When carboxylic acids are treated with $\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}$ or $\text{B}{{\text{r}}_{2}}$ in presence of red phosphorus, the $\alpha $-hydrogen atoms of carboxylic acids are replaced by chlorine or bromine.

Mechanism:
